title: Agent SDK loop: 5 phases from prompt to result

body.overview: The Agent SDK runs a managed loop that handles tool execution automatically. You send a prompt; the SDK drives Claude through evaluate → tool-call → result cycles until Claude has nothing left to do, then delivers a final result with cost and usage stats.

components.name: SystemMessage (init)
components.role: Yielded once at the start with session metadata (session ID, config)

components.name: AssistantMessage
components.role: Yielded each time Claude responds — contains text and/or tool call requests

components.name: UserMessage (tool results)
components.role: Yielded when the SDK feeds tool execution output back to Claude automatically

components.name: ResultMessage
components.role: Yielded once at the end with final text, token usage, cost, and session ID

body.flow: 1. SDK sends prompt + tool definitions + history to Claude → yields SystemMessage. 2. Claude evaluates and responds → SDK yields AssistantMessage (may include tool calls). 3. If tool calls exist: SDK executes them, yields UserMessage with results, feeds results back to Claude. 4. Steps 2–3 repeat (each cycle = one 'turn'). 5. When Claude responds with no tool calls → SDK yields final AssistantMessage + ResultMessage. Loop ends.
